subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 15
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a pouch to carry its young?
questions.explanation: Kangaroos are marsupials, meaning they carry their young, called joeys, in a pouch.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their characteristics.

questions.q: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on our planet.
questions.hint: It covers more than one-third of the Earth's surface.

questions.q: Which of these is a vegetable that is good for your eyes?
questions.explanation: Carrots are rich in beta-carotene, which the body converts into Vitamin A, essential for good vision.

questions.q: What is the name of our home galaxy?
questions.explanation: Our Solar System is located within the Milky Way galaxy.
questions.hint: It has a name that includes a dairy product.

questions.q: Which of these is a non-renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Coal is a fossil fuel and is a non-renewable energy source because it takes millions of years to form.
questions.hint: It is a black rock used to make electricity.

questions.q: Which community helper works in a library?
questions.explanation: A librarian manages the library and helps people find books and information.

questions.q: What gas is essential for humans to breathe?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the gas that humans and many animals need to breathe in to survive.
questions.hint: It makes up about 21% of the Earth's atmosphere.

questions.q: Which of these is a solid state of water?
questions.explanation: Ice is water in its solid form, occurring at temperatures below 0 degrees Celsius.
questions.hint: You put it in drinks to make them cold.

questions.q: What is the speed of light approximately?
questions.explanation: Light travels at about 300,000 kilometers per second in a vacuum.
questions.hint: It is a very, very large number.

questions.q: Which of these is the coldest continent?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the coldest, windiest, and driest continent on Earth.
